key_insight: DeFAb turns defeasible abduction into a rigorously verifiable benchmark, showing that symbolic solvers far outperform frontier LMs on reasoning that must satisfy derivation, conservativity, and minimality checks.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark offers a scalable, contamination-aware testbed for evaluating and training foundation models on logically disciplined theory revision, with exact verifier signals usable for DPO/RLVR/GRPO.
method: Builds instances from 18 knowledge sources, combining taxonomic hierarchies with behavioral property graphs.
Generates three benchmark levels with polynomial-time verifiable gold standards.
Adds hard variants (DeFAb-Hard, CONJURE) to stress-test model robustness and creativity.
evidence_signal: Across 372,648+ instances, the symbolic baseline achieves 100% accuracy in under 50 microseconds, while the best frontier model reaches 65% overall and falls to 23.5% under rendering-robust evaluation.
watch_item: Because performance varies strongly with surface rendering and chain-of-thought prompting, any deployment or comparison should control formatting and robustness conditions carefully.
paper_title: DeFAb: A Verifiable Benchmark for Defeasible Abduction in Foundation Models
